name: ruby-http-cookie
title: ruby-http-cookie
summary: Ruby library to handle HTTP Cookies based on RFC 6265
description:
Ruby library to handle HTTP Cookies based on RFC 6265
HTTP::Cookie is a Ruby library to handle HTTP Cookies based on RFC
6265.  It has with security, standards compliance and compatibility
in mind, to behave just the same as today's major web browsers.  It
has builtin support for the legacy cookies.txt and the latest
cookies.sqlite formats of Mozilla Firefox, and its modular API makes
it easy to add support for a new backend store.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-http-cookie because Ruby library to handle HTTP Cookies based on RFC 6265.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-http-cookie as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-http-cookie with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-http-cookie --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
